jobs.title: Data Analyst (Entry-Level / Junior)
jobs.description:
Data Analyst (Entry-Level / Junior)

Role Description Support business and operational decision-making by collecting, organizing, analyzing, and interpreting data from multiple sources to generate meaningful insights and recommendations. Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, and data visualizations that provide stakeholders with clear visibility into business performance, operational trends, customer behavior, and key performance indicators. Perform data validation, cleansing, and quality assurance activities to ensure the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of datasets. Analyze large datasets to identify patterns, opportunities, risks, and areas for improvement, and communicate findings through clear reports and presentations. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support forecasting, performance measurement, process optimization, and strategic initiatives that contribute to organizational growth and operational excellence. Assist in maintaining data integrity and supporting data-driven decision-making across the organization.
Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Business Administration, Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field. Strong analytical, critical-thinking, and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail. Proficiency in spreadsheets, data analysis tools, reporting platforms, and data visualization software. Familiarity with SQL, database concepts, statistical methodologies, and data management practices is advantageous. Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to translate complex data into clear and actionable insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Strong organizational and time-management capabilities with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines effectively. Knowledge of business intelligence, reporting frameworks, data governance, forecasting, performance measurement, KPI analysis, and process improvement principles is highly beneficial. Experience with dashboard development, business intelligence tools, reporting automation, and data-driven business analysis is considered an advantage.

jobs.title: Data analyst
jobs.description:
About The Company

FocusKPI is a leading talent solutions provider dedicated to connecting innovative companies with exceptional professionals across various industries. Our mission is to empower organizations by delivering tailored staffing and consulting services that drive business growth and operational excellence. With a strong commitment to integrity, quality, and client satisfaction, FocusKPI has established a reputation for excellence in talent acquisition and workforce solutions. We pride ourselves on fostering a collaborative environment that values diversity, continuous learning, and innovative thinking, enabling us to meet the evolving needs of our clients in a dynamic global marketplace.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly skilled Marketing Operations Analyst to join our client's high-tech SaaS company as part of the Marketing Operations & Technology team. In this technical and strategic role, you will be instrumental in shaping and executing the company's data strategy to optimize marketing efforts. Your primary responsibilities will include managing data governance, ensuring compliance with privacy regulations such as GDPR and CCPA, and enabling precise audience targeting for marketing campaigns. This role offers an exciting opportunity for a detail-oriented professional with a passion for data analysis and a knack for solving complex problems in a fast-paced environment. You will work extensively with large data sets, writing complex SQL queries, and utilizing customer data platforms to build and activate targeted audiences, ultimately supporting the company's strategic marketing initiatives.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Marketing, or a related field.
Proven experience in marketing operations, data analysis, or a similar role within a SaaS or technology environment.
Strong proficiency in SQL, with the ability to write complex queries to extract and manipulate large datasets.
Experience working with Customer Data Platforms (CDPs) or similar marketing technology tools.
Solid understanding of data governance principles and privacy regulations such as GDPR and CCPA.
Excellent analytical skills with a keen eye for detail and a methodical approach to problem-solving.
Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks effectively, and thrive in ambiguous situations.
Strong communication skills to collaborate with cross-functional teams and present insights clearly.

Responsibilities

Develop and implement audience segmentation strategies using advanced SQL queries and data manipulation techniques to support targeted marketing campaigns.
Manage and improve data quality, flow, and usability across multiple systems, ensuring consistency and accuracy.
Collaborate with marketing, data engineering, and compliance teams to integrate data sources and streamline data workflows.
Ensure all marketing data practices align with privacy regulations such as GDPR and CCPA, maintaining compliance and mitigating risks.
Build and activate audiences through the customer data platform, enabling personalized marketing initiatives.
Monitor data governance policies and recommend enhancements to improve data accessibility and integrity.
Assist in the development of reporting dashboards and data visualizations to track marketing performance metrics.
Stay current with industry trends and emerging technologies related to marketing operations and data management.

Benefits

Our client offers a competitive compensation package, including comprehensive health benefits, retirement plans, and paid time off. Employees have access to ongoing professional development opportunities, including training programs and industry certifications. The company fosters a collaborative and innovative work environment, encouraging continuous learning and growth. Flexible work arrangements and a focus on work-life balance are integral to the company's culture. Additionally, employees benefit from exposure to cutting-edge marketing technologies and data strategies, providing a stimulating environment for career advancement.

Equal Opportunity

FocusKPI and our clients are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workplace. We provide equal employment opportunities to all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected status. We believe that diversity fosters innovation and drives success, and we are dedicated to maintaining a work environment where everyone feels valued and respected.

jobs.title: Senior Environmental Scientist/Specialist - Restoration Crew Leader | South St. Paul, MN
jobs.description:
Company:
Davey Resource Group, Inc.

Locations:
South St. Paul, MN

Additional Locations:
NA

Work Site:
On Site

Req ID:
225105

Position Overview

Davey Resource Group, a Davey Company, has opportunities for an experienced Environmental Scientist/ Restoration Specialist. Davey Resource Group is a leader in the natural resource and forestry consulting industries throughout the US.

Job Duties

The Environmental Specialist will provide our government, utility, commercial, private and not-for-profit clients with the services and solutions needed to efficiently and effectively manage natural resources and comply with policies.

Selected candidates will work collaboratively as part of a team. Applicants should be willing to lead and train fellow team members on all aspects of field work. Communicate with clients professionally. Position involves a variety of work, including invasive plant species control, native plant and seed installation, shoreline stabilization, and native habitat restoration (prairie, wetland and woodland).

The majority of work is outdoors, often in inclement weather and in rugged terrain, and may involve using heavy equipment, such as spray rigs, backpack sprayers, augers, chain saws, mowers, etc.

Work with the project manager to understand the contract needs and specifications of clients and their customers and develop appropriate solutions.
Inspect a wide range of habitats, both natural and disturbed, and assess their condition, health and functionality according to client specifications.
Identify local species, understand their typical characteristics and recognize common diseases and pests.
Train and mentor teammates.

Qualifications

Education and Qualifications:

An individual must be able to perform each essential job duty consistently and satisfactorily. The requirements listed are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required.

Possession of an Minnesota Department of Agriculture Certified Pesticide Applicators License is required.
Prescribed burn experience and S130/S190 certification.
Identify invasive species across a range of habitats.
Training with chain saw, brush saw, and 4x4 ATV/UTV, tractor, and skid steer.
A degree in biology, botany, ecology, conservation, environmental science, or related fields.
3 to 5 years of experience in ecological restoration, minimum 2 years of experience leading crews of 1-4 people.
Natural resource consulting experience preferred.
Ability to read construction plans and specifications to execute tasks and assign work to crew members preferred.
Ability and willingness to travel.
Driver’s license and clean driving record are required.
Ability to obtain Class A CDL within 6 months.
Experience with heavy equipment operation including skid steers, dingos, and tractors.
Experience with native plant installation, seeding, and erosion control best management practices.
Ability to work independently when not leading crews.

Additional Information

40-50 hours per week, Monday-Friday with some Saturdays optional.
Starting hourly wage: $22 - $27 per hour based on experience.

What We Offer:*

Paid time off and paid holidays.
Opportunities for advancement.
All job specific equipment and safety gear provided.
401(k) retirement savings plan with a company match.
Employee-owned company and discounted stock purchase options.
Group Health Plan.
Employee Referral Bonus Program: Send-A-Friend.
Locations throughout the United States in major cities and desirable areas.
Career Development Program supported by industry expert safety specialists and skilled trainers.
The Davey Tree Family Scholarship for children of employees.
Charitable matching gift program.
All listed benefits available to eligible employees.

Company Overview

DAVEY RESOURCE GROUP
, Inc., headquartered in Kent, Ohio has opportunities for persons interested in the field of urban forestry. We are seeking teamwork-oriented individuals with excellent written and oral communication skills. Candidates should possess a professional work ethic with attention to detail and safety. Davey Resource Group provides a wide range of natural resource consulting services to clients in the private and public sectors, including urban forestry consulting and tree inventories, urban forest canopy assessments, and urban forestry management planning.

Divisional Overview

The Davey Tree Expert Company is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to protected class, including race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national or ethnic origin, marital or familial status, disability, status as a protected veteran, status as an Aboriginal or Indigenous person, or other classification protected by law.

Davey Resource Group, Inc., is a leader in the natural resource, environmental, and forestry consulting industries throughout the United States. The Davey Tree Expert Company provides research-driven tree services, grounds maintenance and environmental consulting for residential, utility, commercial and environmental partners in the U.S. and Canada. We care about our clients, each other and the world around us. We offer the resources, size and stability of a big company while maintaining the culture, entrepreneurial spirit and feel of a small one. We invest in our employees by offering industry-leading training, technology and benefits that lead to a rewarding and safe work experience at all levels. Wherever you want to grow your career, there’s a place for you at Davey. To learn more, visit Davey.com.

Accommodations:
If requested by employee or otherwise as required by law, reasonable accommodations will be made to enable employees with disabilities to perform essential job functions.

If you need assistance at any time, please contact us at 1-877-411-7601 or at
Recruiting@davey.com.

Project Data Analyst (Entry / Junior Level)

Role Description Support project teams by collecting, organizing, analyzing, and interpreting data to improve project planning, execution, and overall performance. Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, and data visualizations that provide stakeholders with actionable insights into project schedules, budgets, resource allocation, risks, milestones, and key performance indicators. Monitor project metrics, identify trends, evaluate project outcomes, and recommend opportunities for process optimization and operational improvement. Assist with forecasting, performance tracking, data validation, and reporting activities to ensure the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of project information. Collaborate with project managers and cross-functional teams to support data-driven decision-making, enhance operational efficiency, and contribute to the successful delivery of projects and strategic initiatives across the organization. Support project governance activities by maintaining accurate documentation, preparing performance reports, and ensuring effective monitoring of project progress throughout the project lifecycle.
Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Business Administration, Economics, Information Systems, Engineering, Project Management, or a related field. Strong analytical, critical-thinking, and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail. Proficiency in spreadsheets, reporting tools, data analysis methodologies, and data visualization platforms. Familiarity with SQL, database concepts, statistical techniques, project performance measurement, and business reporting practices is advantageous. Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to translate complex data into meaningful business insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Strong organizational and time-management capabilities with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines effectively. Knowledge of forecasting, process improvement methodologies, project management principles, business intelligence concepts, KPI reporting, performance analytics, and project governance practices is highly beneficial. Experience with project reporting tools, dashboard development, and project lifecycle management processes is considered an advantage.

jobs.title: Data Analyst - Data & Analytics
jobs.description:
Title:

Data Analyst - Data & Analytics

Company:

Everest Global Services, Inc.

Job Category:

Analytics

Job Description:

About Everest:

Everest is a global leader in risk management, rooted in a rich, 50+ year heritage of enabling businesses to survive and thrive, and economies to function and flourish. We are underwriters of risk, growth, progress and opportunity. We are a global team focused on disciplined capital allocation and long-term value creation for all stakeholders, who care deeply about our impact on communities and the wider world.

Data Analyst – Data & Analytics

Join Everest’s Data & Analytics team as a Data Analyst, where you will play a key role in advancing the firm’s enterprise data strategy.

This role sits at the intersection of business and technology, combining hands-on data analysis, data solutioning, data delivery, and stakeholder engagement to enable high-quality reporting and analytics across the organization.

What You’ll Do

Partner with technology teams to design and deliver scalable data solutions that provide high-quality, reliable data for business use
Drive adoption and business value from data, analytics, and BI solutions across functions
Translate business needs into clear data and technology requirements
Collaborate across business units to standardize and optimize enterprise data assets
Support data governance and data quality initiatives in close partnership with Technology
Act as a subject matter expert (SME) for business data inquiries
Perform ad hoc analysis by extracting, transforming, and modeling data from multiple sources
Validate and sign off on data deliverables to ensure accuracy, consistency, and adherence to standards
Contribute to Agile delivery of data initiatives in partnership with Technology teams

What You Bring

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, or a related technical field
3+ years of experience in data analytics or a related role
Strong understanding of core data concepts, including:
Data modeling (dimensional modeling)
ETL processes
Data warehousing
Data governance frameworks
Structured and unstructured data

Familiarity with metadata management, data lineage, and business glossaries
Advanced SQL skills and strong analytical capabilities
Intermediate to advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel
Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to work across business and technology stakeholders
Excellent communication skills, including the ability to translate complex data concepts into business-friendly insights

Nice to Have

Experience in insurance, reinsurance, or financial services
Exposure to modern data platforms and cloud-based analytics environments
Experience supporting data governance initiatives

The base salary range for this position is $88,000 - $125,000 annually. The offered rate of compensation will be based on individual education, experience, qualifications and work location. All offers include access to a variety of benefits to employees, including health insurance coverage, an employee wellness program, life and disability insurance, 401k match, retirement savings plan, paid holidays and paid time off (PTO).

What if I don’t meet every requirement? At Everest we are dedicated to building an inclusive and authentic workplace. So, if you are excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every element in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles. Please let us know if you need any accommodations throughout the application or interview process.

Our Culture

At Everest, our purpose is to provide the world with protection. We help clients and businesses thrive, fuel global economies, and create sustainable value for our colleagues, shareholders and the communities that we serve. We also pride ourselves on having a unique and inclusive culture which is driven by a unified set of values and behaviors. Click here to learn more about our culture.

Our Values are the guiding principles that inform our decisions, actions and behaviors. They are an expression of our culture and an integral part of how we work: Talent. Thoughtful assumption of risk. Execution. Efficiency. Humility. Leadership. Collaboration. Inclusion and Belonging.
Our Colleague Behaviors define how we operate and interact with each other no matter our location, level or function: Respect everyone. Pursue better. Lead by example. Own our outcomes. Win together.

All colleagues are held accountable to upholding and supporting our values and behaviors across the company. This includes day to day interactions with fellow colleagues, and the global communities we serve.

Type:

Regular

Time Type:

Full time

Primary Location:

Warren, NJ

Additional Locations:

New York, NY

Everest is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion or creed, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin or ancestry, citizenship, genetics, physical or mental disability, age, marital status, civil union status, family or parental status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. As part of this commitment, Everest will ensure that persons with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodations. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and/or to receive other benefits and privileges of employment, please contact Everest Benefits at everestbenefits@everestglobal.com.

Everest U.S. Privacy Notice | Everest (everestglobal.com)

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

We are supporting our client on a search for a Data Analyst who will sit at the center of how the company makes decisions. This is a high-leverage seat for an analyst who loves SQL, dashboards, and turning messy data into clear answers.
You will partner with every function in the business and report directly to the head of data. You will work hybrid out of a great Atlanta office.

COMPENSATION

$70,000 - $95,000 per year, depending on experience.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

- Build and maintain dashboards and reports across business functions
- Partner with marketing, sales, product, and finance on data requests
- Write SQL to extract, transform, and validate data
- Run ad hoc analyses and turn findings into clear recommendations
- Document data definitions and improve data quality
- Help define KPIs and instrument tracking with engineering
- Support quarterly business reviews and board prep
- Build automated reporting and alerting

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

- 1 to 3 years of analytics, BI, or data experience
- Comfort writing SQL and using a BI tool like Looker, Tableau, or Mode
- Strong analytical thinking and clear written communication
- Curiosity and willingness to learn new domains quickly
- Hybrid presence in the Atlanta office at least two days per week
- Bachelors degree preferred but not required

NICE TO HAVE

- Familiarity with dbt, Snowflake, BigQuery, or Redshift
- Light Python or R for analysis
- Experience with experimentation or A/B testing

BENEFITS & PERKS

- Hybrid work environment with a modern Atlanta office
- Competitive salary with annual reviews
- Medical, dental, and vision insurance
- 401(k) plan with employer match
- Generous paid time off and paid holidays
- Annual learning and development stipend
- Commuter benefits

WHY APPLY

You will work on real business problems, see the impact of your work, and grow into senior analyst or analytics engineering roles.

ABOUT CAREERSCAPE

Careerscape is a staffing firm matching exceptional talent with growing companies across industries.

APPLICATION NOTICE

Apply via the external link to be considered.

EQUAL OPPORTUNITY STATEMENT

Careerscape is an Equal Opportunity Employer. M/F/Disability/Veterans.

jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
Project Coordinator

The Gregory Marcinski Project is seeking a volunteer Project Coordinator to help organize and strengthen a growing advocacy and public education effort centered on Gregory J. Marcinski, while also supporting a broader long-term mission for people facing life without parole or natural life sentences.

Gregory J. Marcinski is the principal figure and namesake of the project. He is an award-winning incarcerated journalist and Senior Writer with the Prison Journalism Project. His public work focuses on prison life, institutional systems, rehabilitation, faith, human dignity, and the realities of long-term incarceration.

The project exists to support Gregory’s public writing, advocacy, personal transformation, lawful sentencing alignment, and clemency-related work. It is not about excusing harm or avoiding accountability. It is about supporting a serious, truthful, and human-centered conversation about accountability, rehabilitation, redemption, proportionality, and second chances.

Although Gregory is the immediate focus, the long-term goal is broader. The Gregory Marcinski Project seeks to help develop an organized model of advocacy, documentation, outreach, public education, and support that may eventually assist others facing life without parole or natural life sentences.

The project includes several connected areas of work:

The Co-Terminus Initiative, focused on Kentucky and federal sentence-alignment advocacy
The Federal Initiative, focused on future clemency and sentence-reduction support
Public education through Gregory’s writing, social media, the project website, LinkedIn, Facebook, Bluesky, Quora, and Substack
Outreach to supporters, journalists, advocates, faith communities, reform organizations, and people with Kentucky connections
Organization of documents, timelines, evidence, correspondence, public materials, and volunteer activity
Development of a long-term advocacy model for people serving or facing life without parole or natural life sentences

The Project Coordinator will help keep this work organized, focused, and moving forward. This role is ideal for someone who is reliable, detail-oriented, thoughtful, and comfortable helping turn many moving pieces into clear next steps.

The Project Coordinator does not need to be an attorney, nonprofit executive, campaign professional, or criminal justice expert. The most important qualities are consistency, discretion, clear communication, organization, and follow-through.

Primary responsibilities may include:

Maintaining a clear list of current priorities, open tasks, deadlines, and next steps
Helping organize work across volunteer recruitment, public education, social media, website updates, Kentucky outreach, clemency support, and research organization
Breaking larger goals into smaller, manageable tasks
Tracking what has been completed, what is pending, and what needs follow-up
Helping organize documents, links, notes, drafts, contacts, timelines, and project materials
Supporting volunteer coordination and helping match volunteers with appropriate tasks
Helping prepare simple volunteer messages, onboarding notes, and task descriptions
Tracking outreach to supporters, advocacy groups, faith communities, journalists, Kentucky contacts, and potential partners
Helping keep public messaging respectful, accurate, consistent, and human-centered
Supporting the organization of materials connected to Gregory’s public writing and journalism
Helping maintain systems that may later support the project’s broader mission for others facing life without parole or natural life sentences

The Gregory Marcinski Project has a specific need for organized support connected to Kentucky. The Project Coordinator may help organize materials related to Kentucky outreach, Kentucky clemency support, the Co-Terminus Initiative, supporter packets, timelines, letters, research files, public-facing summaries, and follow-up lists.

This is not a legal role. The Project Coordinator will not provide legal advice or promise legal outcomes. The role is to help organize, track, coordinate, and support advocacy-related work.

The ideal volunteer will be:

Organized and reliable
Good at tracking details
Comfortable with lists, documents, notes, and follow-up
Able to communicate clearly and respectfully
Patient with a growing project that is still building structure
Comfortable working remotely
Interested in redemption, second chances, restorative justice, criminal justice reform, public education, faith-based service, or human dignity
Able to handle sensitive information with discretion
Willing to help create order from many moving pieces

Helpful experience may include project coordination, administrative support, volunteer coordination, nonprofit work, advocacy, communications, social media planning, research organization, writing, editing, journalism, faith-based outreach, criminal justice reform, clemency support, or use of shared documents and spreadsheets.

This is a flexible volunteer role, but consistency matters. A few focused hours each week can make a meaningful difference if the person is reliable, communicative, and able to help track next steps.

This role matters because serious advocacy requires more than passion. It requires organization, communication, documentation, consistency, and follow-through. The Project Coordinator will help turn concern into action, action into progress, and progress into lasting structure.

If you are interested in volunteering as Project Coordinator, please visit:

thegregorymarcinskiproject.org

You may also reach out with a brief note explaining why you are interested, any relevant experience or skills, how much time you may realistically be able to offer, and whether you are most interested in coordination, outreach, communications, research, Kentucky support, Gregory’s public writing, long-term advocacy model building, or general project organization.

The Gregory Marcinski Project welcomes volunteers who believe that people are more than the worst thing they have ever done, and that accountability, rehabilitation, redemption, lawful sentencing alignment, and human dignity deserve a serious place in public conversation.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Entry level

Employment type

Volunteer

Industries

Civic and Social Organizations

jobs.title: PROGRAM COORDINATOR – WORKFORCE & OPERATIONS
jobs.description:
PROGRAM COORDINATOR – WORKFORCE & OPERATIONS (RALEIGH-DURHAM, NC)

Location:
Remote/Hybrid (with some in-person based on program needs in Raleigh-Durham, NC)

COMPANY DESCRIPTION

Entryway is a national 501(c)(3) organization that transitions individuals and families out of homelessness and into economic self-sufficiency. We partner with the $1.3 trillion multifamily housing industry to create pathways to stable employment, housing, and long-term opportunity.

As a catalyst for change, Entryway brings together property management companies, employers, service providers, and community partners to create a coordinated ecosystem of support. With more than 200 partner organizations nationwide, we deliver a proven, industry-powered model at the intersection of housing and employment.

Unlike traditional nonprofits that focus on either housing or employment, Entryway delivers both. Participants gain access to stable jobs with a living wage, benefits, and on-site housing opportunities through our property management partners. Combined with training and individualized support, this integrated approach creates the conditions for long-term success.

In North Carolina, the Program Coordinator in Raleigh plays an important role in supporting program success by building relationships with partners, guiding participants, and helping ensure strong, consistent outcomes, including job placement and retention.

POSITION OVERVIEW

Entryway is seeking a highly organized, proactive, and detail-oriented Program Coordinator to lead and support our workforce program in the Raleigh-Durham area.

This role combines program coordination, participant support, and operational execution. The Program Coordinator supports the full lifecycle of the Raleigh program—from recruiting participants to coordinating and managing training programs, and supporting placement and retention outcomes—while also assisting with program operations.

The ideal candidate is organized, communicates effectively, and takes initiative in both participant-facing and behind-the-scenes work.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Program Coordination & Support

- Coordinate and manage multi-week training programs, including securing instructors, coordinating logistics, managing schedules and communications, and ensuring a well-organized participant experience 
- Manage recruitment, screening, and onboarding of participants 
- Ensure participants stay engaged and on track throughout the program 
- Support placement efforts and participant follow-up 
- Track key outcomes, including placements and retention 

Recruitment, Outreach & Pipeline

- Build and maintain relationships with community partners and referral networks 
- Conduct outreach and communicate program expectations to prospective participants 
- Maintain a strong pipeline for upcoming cohorts 

Participant Engagement & Support

- Build positive, supportive relationships with participants 
- Provide consistent communication, encouragement, and accountability 
- Assist with resumes, job applications, and next steps 
- Support individuals facing barriers to employment or housing stability 

Employer Engagement & Placement

- Build and maintain relationships with employer partners 
- Connect participants to job opportunities aligned with their goals 
- Assist with interviews and job readiness preparation 
- Support early retention follow-up 

Data Tracking & Reporting

- Maintain accurate tracking of participant data, attendance, and outcomes 
- Keep systems and documentation current and organized 
- Support program reporting requirements 

Program Operations & Administrative Support

- Support day-to-day program operations and logistics 
- Assist with scheduling, coordination, and internal communications 
- Maintain tracking tools and program materials 
- Draft communications and support outreach efforts 

Ownership & Initiative

- Manage multiple responsibilities with strong attention to detail 
- Take initiative and follow through on tasks 
- Communicate proactively with team members and partners

This role supports individuals working toward employment and housing stability by helping ensure participants are prepared, connected to opportunities, and successful in achieving and maintaining employment.

jobs.title: PROGRAM COORDINATOR – WORKFORCE & OPERATIONS
jobs.description:
PROGRAM COORDINATOR – WORKFORCE & OPERATIONS (CHARLOTTE, NC)

Location:
Hybrid (with regular in-person presence based on program needs in Charlotte, NC)

COMPANY DESCRIPTION

Entryway is a national 501(c)(3) organization that transitions individuals and families out of homelessness into economic self-sufficiency. We partner with the $1.3 trillion multifamily housing industry to create pathways to stable employment, housing, and long-term opportunity.
As a catalyst for change, Entryway brings together property management companies, employers, service providers, and community partners to create a coordinated ecosystem of support. With more than 200 partner organizations nationwide, we deliver a proven, industry-powered model at the intersection of housing and employment.
Unlike traditional nonprofits that focus on either housing or employment, Entryway delivers both. Participants access stable jobs with a living wage, benefits, and on-site housing opportunities through our partners. Combined with training and individualized support, this approach creates the conditions for long-term success.
In North Carolina, the Program Coordinator in Charlotte plays a critical role in implementing this model—building partner relationships, guiding participants from recruitment through placement, and ensuring strong, measurable outcomes, including job placement and retention.

POSITION OVERVIEW

Entryway is seeking a highly organized, proactive, and detail-oriented Program Coordinator to lead and execute our workforce program in Charlotte.
This high-impact role is responsible for the full lifecycle of the program—from recruiting participants, to coordinating training programs, to driving placement and retention outcomes—while maintaining accurate tracking and supporting program operations.
The ideal candidate is organized, communicates effectively, takes ownership of results, and is comfortable balancing administrative work with participant and partner engagement in a fast-paced environment.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Program Coordination & Execution

- Coordinate and manage multi-week training programs, including securing instructors, managing logistics and schedules, and ensuring a high-quality participant experience 
- Build and maintain a pipeline of qualified participants through recruitment, screening, and onboarding 
- Ensure participants remain engaged, accountable, and on track through program completion 
Placement & Employer Engagement

- Build and maintain strong relationships with employer partners 
- Connect participants to job opportunities aligned with their skills and goals 
- Coordinate interviews and hiring processes 
- Drive placement outcomes and support early retention 
Participant Support

- Build strong, trust-based relationships with participants 
- Provide consistent communication, guidance, and accountability 
- Support resumes, applications, interview preparation, and next steps 
- Help address barriers to successful program completion 
Data Tracking & Reporting

- Maintain accurate, real-time tracking of participant data, attendance, and outcomes 
- Ensure documentation is complete and reporting requirements are met 
- Maintain organized, up-to-date systems and records 
Program Operations & Administrative Support

- Coordinate scheduling, communications, and program logistics 
- Maintain tracking tools and program materials 
- Draft communications and support outreach efforts 
- Assist with internal coordination as needed 
Ownership & Initiative

- Take ownership of program performance from recruitment through outcomes 
- Manage priorities with strong attention to detail 
- Identify challenges early and take initiative to resolve them 
- Follow through consistently and ensure accountability 

REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

- 2+ years of experience in program coordination, workforce development, or a related field 
- Strong organizational skills and attention to detail 
- Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines 
- Strong written and verbal communication skills 
- Experience with spreadsheets, databases, or tracking systems (Excel, Google Sheets, CRM, etc.) 

SUCCESS TRAITS

- Ownership mindset and strong follow-through 
- Proactive, solutions-oriented approach 
- Strong communicator across diverse audiences 
- Able to balance administration with participant engagement 
- Builds trust with individuals facing employment barriers 
- Highly organized and detail-oriented 
- Motivated to deliver measurable outcomes 

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience in workforce development, nonprofit programs, or social services 
- Experience working with underserved populations 
- Experience with job placement or employer engagement 
- Familiarity with CRM or case management systems 

This role directly impacts participants’ ability to achieve employment and housing stability by ensuring successful program completion, job placement, and continued employment.

IMPORTANT NOTE
This role requires a high level of ownership, organization, and accountability.

You will be responsible for ensuring the program runs effectively and consistently produces strong placement and retention outcomes while maintaining clear and accurate tracking.

Salary: $58,000–$60,000

new_jobs.description:
Data Analyst (Entry-Level / Junior)

Role Description Support business and operational decision-making by collecting, organizing, analyzing, and interpreting data from multiple sources to generate meaningful insights and recommendations. Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, and data visualizations that provide stakeholders with clear visibility into business performance, operational trends, customer behavior, and key performance indicators. Perform data validation, cleansing, and quality assurance activities to ensure the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of datasets. Analyze large datasets to identify patterns, opportunities, risks, and areas for improvement, and communicate findings through clear reports and presentations. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support forecasting, performance measurement, process optimization, and strategic initiatives that contribute to organizational growth and operational excellence. Assist in maintaining data integrity and supporting data-driven decision-making across the organization.
Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Business Administration, Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field. Strong analytical, critical-thinking, and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail. Proficiency in spreadsheets, data analysis tools, reporting platforms, and data visualization software. Familiarity with SQL, database concepts, statistical methodologies, and data management practices is advantageous. Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to translate complex data into clear and actionable insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Strong organizational and time-management capabilities with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines effectively. Knowledge of business intelligence, reporting frameworks, data governance, forecasting, performance measurement, KPI analysis, and process improvement principles is highly beneficial. Experience with dashboard development, business intelligence tools, reporting automation, and data-driven business analysis is considered an advantage.

new_jobs.title: Data analyst
new_jobs.description:
About The Company

FocusKPI is a leading talent solutions provider dedicated to connecting innovative companies with exceptional professionals across various industries. Our mission is to empower organizations by delivering tailored staffing and consulting services that drive business growth and operational excellence. With a strong commitment to integrity, quality, and client satisfaction, FocusKPI has established a reputation for excellence in talent acquisition and workforce solutions. We pride ourselves on fostering a collaborative environment that values diversity, continuous learning, and innovative thinking, enabling us to meet the evolving needs of our clients in a dynamic global marketplace.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly skilled Marketing Operations Analyst to join our client's high-tech SaaS company as part of the Marketing Operations & Technology team. In this technical and strategic role, you will be instrumental in shaping and executing the company's data strategy to optimize marketing efforts. Your primary responsibilities will include managing data governance, ensuring compliance with privacy regulations such as GDPR and CCPA, and enabling precise audience targeting for marketing campaigns. This role offers an exciting opportunity for a detail-oriented professional with a passion for data analysis and a knack for solving complex problems in a fast-paced environment. You will work extensively with large data sets, writing complex SQL queries, and utilizing customer data platforms to build and activate targeted audiences, ultimately supporting the company's strategic marketing initiatives.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Marketing, or a related field.
Proven experience in marketing operations, data analysis, or a similar role within a SaaS or technology environment.
Strong proficiency in SQL, with the ability to write complex queries to extract and manipulate large datasets.
Experience working with Customer Data Platforms (CDPs) or similar marketing technology tools.
Solid understanding of data governance principles and privacy regulations such as GDPR and CCPA.
Excellent analytical skills with a keen eye for detail and a methodical approach to problem-solving.
Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks effectively, and thrive in ambiguous situations.
Strong communication skills to collaborate with cross-functional teams and present insights clearly.

Responsibilities

Develop and implement audience segmentation strategies using advanced SQL queries and data manipulation techniques to support targeted marketing campaigns.
Manage and improve data quality, flow, and usability across multiple systems, ensuring consistency and accuracy.
Collaborate with marketing, data engineering, and compliance teams to integrate data sources and streamline data workflows.
Ensure all marketing data practices align with privacy regulations such as GDPR and CCPA, maintaining compliance and mitigating risks.
Build and activate audiences through the customer data platform, enabling personalized marketing initiatives.
Monitor data governance policies and recommend enhancements to improve data accessibility and integrity.
Assist in the development of reporting dashboards and data visualizations to track marketing performance metrics.
Stay current with industry trends and emerging technologies related to marketing operations and data management.

Benefits

Our client offers a competitive compensation package, including comprehensive health benefits, retirement plans, and paid time off. Employees have access to ongoing professional development opportunities, including training programs and industry certifications. The company fosters a collaborative and innovative work environment, encouraging continuous learning and growth. Flexible work arrangements and a focus on work-life balance are integral to the company's culture. Additionally, employees benefit from exposure to cutting-edge marketing technologies and data strategies, providing a stimulating environment for career advancement.

Equal Opportunity

FocusKPI and our clients are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workplace. We provide equal employment opportunities to all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected status. We believe that diversity fosters innovation and drives success, and we are dedicated to maintaining a work environment where everyone feels valued and respected.

new_jobs.title: Senior Environmental Scientist/Specialist - Restoration Crew Leader | South St. Paul, MN
new_jobs.description:
Company:
Davey Resource Group, Inc.

Locations:
South St. Paul, MN

Additional Locations:
NA

Work Site:
On Site

Req ID:
225105

Position Overview

Davey Resource Group, a Davey Company, has opportunities for an experienced Environmental Scientist/ Restoration Specialist. Davey Resource Group is a leader in the natural resource and forestry consulting industries throughout the US.

Job Duties

The Environmental Specialist will provide our government, utility, commercial, private and not-for-profit clients with the services and solutions needed to efficiently and effectively manage natural resources and comply with policies.

Selected candidates will work collaboratively as part of a team. Applicants should be willing to lead and train fellow team members on all aspects of field work. Communicate with clients professionally. Position involves a variety of work, including invasive plant species control, native plant and seed installation, shoreline stabilization, and native habitat restoration (prairie, wetland and woodland).

The majority of work is outdoors, often in inclement weather and in rugged terrain, and may involve using heavy equipment, such as spray rigs, backpack sprayers, augers, chain saws, mowers, etc.

Work with the project manager to understand the contract needs and specifications of clients and their customers and develop appropriate solutions.
Inspect a wide range of habitats, both natural and disturbed, and assess their condition, health and functionality according to client specifications.
Identify local species, understand their typical characteristics and recognize common diseases and pests.
Train and mentor teammates.

Qualifications

Education and Qualifications:

An individual must be able to perform each essential job duty consistently and satisfactorily. The requirements listed are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required.

Possession of an Minnesota Department of Agriculture Certified Pesticide Applicators License is required.
Prescribed burn experience and S130/S190 certification.
Identify invasive species across a range of habitats.
Training with chain saw, brush saw, and 4x4 ATV/UTV, tractor, and skid steer.
A degree in biology, botany, ecology, conservation, environmental science, or related fields.
3 to 5 years of experience in ecological restoration, minimum 2 years of experience leading crews of 1-4 people.
Natural resource consulting experience preferred.
Ability to read construction plans and specifications to execute tasks and assign work to crew members preferred.
Ability and willingness to travel.
Driver’s license and clean driving record are required.
Ability to obtain Class A CDL within 6 months.
Experience with heavy equipment operation including skid steers, dingos, and tractors.
Experience with native plant installation, seeding, and erosion control best management practices.
Ability to work independently when not leading crews.

Additional Information

40-50 hours per week, Monday-Friday with some Saturdays optional.
Starting hourly wage: $22 - $27 per hour based on experience.

What We Offer:*

Paid time off and paid holidays.
Opportunities for advancement.
All job specific equipment and safety gear provided.
401(k) retirement savings plan with a company match.
Employee-owned company and discounted stock purchase options.
Group Health Plan.
Employee Referral Bonus Program: Send-A-Friend.
Locations throughout the United States in major cities and desirable areas.
Career Development Program supported by industry expert safety specialists and skilled trainers.
The Davey Tree Family Scholarship for children of employees.
Charitable matching gift program.
All listed benefits available to eligible employees.

Company Overview

DAVEY RESOURCE GROUP
, Inc., headquartered in Kent, Ohio has opportunities for persons interested in the field of urban forestry. We are seeking teamwork-oriented individuals with excellent written and oral communication skills. Candidates should possess a professional work ethic with attention to detail and safety. Davey Resource Group provides a wide range of natural resource consulting services to clients in the private and public sectors, including urban forestry consulting and tree inventories, urban forest canopy assessments, and urban forestry management planning.

Divisional Overview

The Davey Tree Expert Company is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to protected class, including race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national or ethnic origin, marital or familial status, disability, status as a protected veteran, status as an Aboriginal or Indigenous person, or other classification protected by law.

Davey Resource Group, Inc., is a leader in the natural resource, environmental, and forestry consulting industries throughout the United States. The Davey Tree Expert Company provides research-driven tree services, grounds maintenance and environmental consulting for residential, utility, commercial and environmental partners in the U.S. and Canada. We care about our clients, each other and the world around us. We offer the resources, size and stability of a big company while maintaining the culture, entrepreneurial spirit and feel of a small one. We invest in our employees by offering industry-leading training, technology and benefits that lead to a rewarding and safe work experience at all levels. Wherever you want to grow your career, there’s a place for you at Davey. To learn more, visit Davey.com.

Accommodations:
If requested by employee or otherwise as required by law, reasonable accommodations will be made to enable employees with disabilities to perform essential job functions.

If you need assistance at any time, please contact us at 1-877-411-7601 or at
Recruiting@davey.com.

new_jobs.title: Data Analyst - Data & Analytics
new_jobs.description:
Title:

Data Analyst - Data & Analytics

Company:

Everest Global Services, Inc.

Job Category:

Analytics

Job Description:

About Everest:

Everest is a global leader in risk management, rooted in a rich, 50+ year heritage of enabling businesses to survive and thrive, and economies to function and flourish. We are underwriters of risk, growth, progress and opportunity. We are a global team focused on disciplined capital allocation and long-term value creation for all stakeholders, who care deeply about our impact on communities and the wider world.

Data Analyst – Data & Analytics

Join Everest’s Data & Analytics team as a Data Analyst, where you will play a key role in advancing the firm’s enterprise data strategy.

This role sits at the intersection of business and technology, combining hands-on data analysis, data solutioning, data delivery, and stakeholder engagement to enable high-quality reporting and analytics across the organization.

What You’ll Do

Partner with technology teams to design and deliver scalable data solutions that provide high-quality, reliable data for business use
Drive adoption and business value from data, analytics, and BI solutions across functions
Translate business needs into clear data and technology requirements
Collaborate across business units to standardize and optimize enterprise data assets
Support data governance and data quality initiatives in close partnership with Technology
Act as a subject matter expert (SME) for business data inquiries
Perform ad hoc analysis by extracting, transforming, and modeling data from multiple sources
Validate and sign off on data deliverables to ensure accuracy, consistency, and adherence to standards
Contribute to Agile delivery of data initiatives in partnership with Technology teams

What You Bring

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, or a related technical field
3+ years of experience in data analytics or a related role
Strong understanding of core data concepts, including:
Data modeling (dimensional modeling)
ETL processes
Data warehousing
Data governance frameworks
Structured and unstructured data

Familiarity with metadata management, data lineage, and business glossaries
Advanced SQL skills and strong analytical capabilities
Intermediate to advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel
Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to work across business and technology stakeholders
Excellent communication skills, including the ability to translate complex data concepts into business-friendly insights

Nice to Have

Experience in insurance, reinsurance, or financial services
Exposure to modern data platforms and cloud-based analytics environments
Experience supporting data governance initiatives

The base salary range for this position is $88,000 - $125,000 annually. The offered rate of compensation will be based on individual education, experience, qualifications and work location. All offers include access to a variety of benefits to employees, including health insurance coverage, an employee wellness program, life and disability insurance, 401k match, retirement savings plan, paid holidays and paid time off (PTO).

What if I don’t meet every requirement? At Everest we are dedicated to building an inclusive and authentic workplace. So, if you are excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every element in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles. Please let us know if you need any accommodations throughout the application or interview process.

Our Culture

At Everest, our purpose is to provide the world with protection. We help clients and businesses thrive, fuel global economies, and create sustainable value for our colleagues, shareholders and the communities that we serve. We also pride ourselves on having a unique and inclusive culture which is driven by a unified set of values and behaviors. Click here to learn more about our culture.

Our Values are the guiding principles that inform our decisions, actions and behaviors. They are an expression of our culture and an integral part of how we work: Talent. Thoughtful assumption of risk. Execution. Efficiency. Humility. Leadership. Collaboration. Inclusion and Belonging.
Our Colleague Behaviors define how we operate and interact with each other no matter our location, level or function: Respect everyone. Pursue better. Lead by example. Own our outcomes. Win together.

All colleagues are held accountable to upholding and supporting our values and behaviors across the company. This includes day to day interactions with fellow colleagues, and the global communities we serve.

Type:

Regular

Time Type:

Full time

Primary Location:

Warren, NJ

Additional Locations:

New York, NY

Everest is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion or creed, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin or ancestry, citizenship, genetics, physical or mental disability, age, marital status, civil union status, family or parental status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. As part of this commitment, Everest will ensure that persons with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodations. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and/or to receive other benefits and privileges of employment, please contact Everest Benefits at everestbenefits@everestglobal.com.

new_jobs.title: Project Coordinator
new_jobs.description:
Project Coordinator

The Gregory Marcinski Project is seeking a volunteer Project Coordinator to help organize and strengthen a growing advocacy and public education effort centered on Gregory J. Marcinski, while also supporting a broader long-term mission for people facing life without parole or natural life sentences.

Gregory J. Marcinski is the principal figure and namesake of the project. He is an award-winning incarcerated journalist and Senior Writer with the Prison Journalism Project. His public work focuses on prison life, institutional systems, rehabilitation, faith, human dignity, and the realities of long-term incarceration.

The project exists to support Gregory’s public writing, advocacy, personal transformation, lawful sentencing alignment, and clemency-related work. It is not about excusing harm or avoiding accountability. It is about supporting a serious, truthful, and human-centered conversation about accountability, rehabilitation, redemption, proportionality, and second chances.

Although Gregory is the immediate focus, the long-term goal is broader. The Gregory Marcinski Project seeks to help develop an organized model of advocacy, documentation, outreach, public education, and support that may eventually assist others facing life without parole or natural life sentences.

The project includes several connected areas of work:

The Co-Terminus Initiative, focused on Kentucky and federal sentence-alignment advocacy
The Federal Initiative, focused on future clemency and sentence-reduction support
Public education through Gregory’s writing, social media, the project website, LinkedIn, Facebook, Bluesky, Quora, and Substack
Outreach to supporters, journalists, advocates, faith communities, reform organizations, and people with Kentucky connections
Organization of documents, timelines, evidence, correspondence, public materials, and volunteer activity
Development of a long-term advocacy model for people serving or facing life without parole or natural life sentences

The Project Coordinator will help keep this work organized, focused, and moving forward. This role is ideal for someone who is reliable, detail-oriented, thoughtful, and comfortable helping turn many moving pieces into clear next steps.

The Project Coordinator does not need to be an attorney, nonprofit executive, campaign professional, or criminal justice expert. The most important qualities are consistency, discretion, clear communication, organization, and follow-through.

Primary responsibilities may include:

Maintaining a clear list of current priorities, open tasks, deadlines, and next steps
Helping organize work across volunteer recruitment, public education, social media, website updates, Kentucky outreach, clemency support, and research organization
Breaking larger goals into smaller, manageable tasks
Tracking what has been completed, what is pending, and what needs follow-up
Helping organize documents, links, notes, drafts, contacts, timelines, and project materials
Supporting volunteer coordination and helping match volunteers with appropriate tasks
Helping prepare simple volunteer messages, onboarding notes, and task descriptions
Tracking outreach to supporters, advocacy groups, faith communities, journalists, Kentucky contacts, and potential partners
Helping keep public messaging respectful, accurate, consistent, and human-centered
Supporting the organization of materials connected to Gregory’s public writing and journalism
Helping maintain systems that may later support the project’s broader mission for others facing life without parole or natural life sentences

The Gregory Marcinski Project has a specific need for organized support connected to Kentucky. The Project Coordinator may help organize materials related to Kentucky outreach, Kentucky clemency support, the Co-Terminus Initiative, supporter packets, timelines, letters, research files, public-facing summaries, and follow-up lists.

This is not a legal role. The Project Coordinator will not provide legal advice or promise legal outcomes. The role is to help organize, track, coordinate, and support advocacy-related work.

The ideal volunteer will be:

Organized and reliable
Good at tracking details
Comfortable with lists, documents, notes, and follow-up
Able to communicate clearly and respectfully
Patient with a growing project that is still building structure
Comfortable working remotely
Interested in redemption, second chances, restorative justice, criminal justice reform, public education, faith-based service, or human dignity
Able to handle sensitive information with discretion
Willing to help create order from many moving pieces

Helpful experience may include project coordination, administrative support, volunteer coordination, nonprofit work, advocacy, communications, social media planning, research organization, writing, editing, journalism, faith-based outreach, criminal justice reform, clemency support, or use of shared documents and spreadsheets.

This is a flexible volunteer role, but consistency matters. A few focused hours each week can make a meaningful difference if the person is reliable, communicative, and able to help track next steps.

This role matters because serious advocacy requires more than passion. It requires organization, communication, documentation, consistency, and follow-through. The Project Coordinator will help turn concern into action, action into progress, and progress into lasting structure.

If you are interested in volunteering as Project Coordinator, please visit:

thegregorymarcinskiproject.org

You may also reach out with a brief note explaining why you are interested, any relevant experience or skills, how much time you may realistically be able to offer, and whether you are most interested in coordination, outreach, communications, research, Kentucky support, Gregory’s public writing, long-term advocacy model building, or general project organization.

The Gregory Marcinski Project welcomes volunteers who believe that people are more than the worst thing they have ever done, and that accountability, rehabilitation, redemption, lawful sentencing alignment, and human dignity deserve a serious place in public conversation.

new_jobs.title: PROGRAM COORDINATOR – WORKFORCE & OPERATIONS
new_jobs.description:
PROGRAM COORDINATOR – WORKFORCE & OPERATIONS (RALEIGH-DURHAM, NC)

Location:
Remote/Hybrid (with some in-person based on program needs in Raleigh-Durham, NC)

COMPANY DESCRIPTION

Entryway is a national 501(c)(3) organization that transitions individuals and families out of homelessness and into economic self-sufficiency. We partner with the $1.3 trillion multifamily housing industry to create pathways to stable employment, housing, and long-term opportunity.

As a catalyst for change, Entryway brings together property management companies, employers, service providers, and community partners to create a coordinated ecosystem of support. With more than 200 partner organizations nationwide, we deliver a proven, industry-powered model at the intersection of housing and employment.

Unlike traditional nonprofits that focus on either housing or employment, Entryway delivers both. Participants gain access to stable jobs with a living wage, benefits, and on-site housing opportunities through our property management partners. Combined with training and individualized support, this integrated approach creates the conditions for long-term success.

In North Carolina, the Program Coordinator in Raleigh plays an important role in supporting program success by building relationships with partners, guiding participants, and helping ensure strong, consistent outcomes, including job placement and retention.

POSITION OVERVIEW

Entryway is seeking a highly organized, proactive, and detail-oriented Program Coordinator to lead and support our workforce program in the Raleigh-Durham area.

This role combines program coordination, participant support, and operational execution. The Program Coordinator supports the full lifecycle of the Raleigh program—from recruiting participants to coordinating and managing training programs, and supporting placement and retention outcomes—while also assisting with program operations.

The ideal candidate is organized, communicates effectively, and takes initiative in both participant-facing and behind-the-scenes work.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Program Coordination & Support

- Coordinate and manage multi-week training programs, including securing instructors, coordinating logistics, managing schedules and communications, and ensuring a well-organized participant experience 
- Manage recruitment, screening, and onboarding of participants 
- Ensure participants stay engaged and on track throughout the program 
- Support placement efforts and participant follow-up 
- Track key outcomes, including placements and retention 

Recruitment, Outreach & Pipeline

- Build and maintain relationships with community partners and referral networks 
- Conduct outreach and communicate program expectations to prospective participants 
- Maintain a strong pipeline for upcoming cohorts 

Participant Engagement & Support

- Build positive, supportive relationships with participants 
- Provide consistent communication, encouragement, and accountability 
- Assist with resumes, job applications, and next steps 
- Support individuals facing barriers to employment or housing stability 

Employer Engagement & Placement

- Build and maintain relationships with employer partners 
- Connect participants to job opportunities aligned with their goals 
- Assist with interviews and job readiness preparation 
- Support early retention follow-up 

Data Tracking & Reporting

- Maintain accurate tracking of participant data, attendance, and outcomes 
- Keep systems and documentation current and organized 
- Support program reporting requirements 

Program Operations & Administrative Support

- Support day-to-day program operations and logistics 
- Assist with scheduling, coordination, and internal communications 
- Maintain tracking tools and program materials 
- Draft communications and support outreach efforts 

Ownership & Initiative

- Manage multiple responsibilities with strong attention to detail 
- Take initiative and follow through on tasks 
- Communicate proactively with team members and partners 
- Contribute to an organized, well-run program environment 

REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

- 2+ years of experience in program coordination, administration, or a related field 
- Strong organizational skills and attention to detail 
- Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines 
- Strong written and verbal communication skills 
- Experience with spreadsheets, databases, or tracking systems (Excel, Google Sheets, CRM, etc.) 

SUCCESS TRAITS

- Reliable, organized, and follows through 
- Takes initiative and works independently 
- Communicates effectively with diverse audiences 
- Balances administrative work with participant engagement 
- Builds trust with individuals facing barriers to employment 
- Professional, patient, and solution-oriented 
- Motivated to support participant success and program outcomes 

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience in workforce development, nonprofit programs, or social services 
- Experience working with underserved populations 
- Experience supporting training, placement, or participant services 
- Familiarity with CRM or case management systems 

This role supports individuals working toward employment and housing stability by helping ensure participants are prepared, connected to opportunities, and successful in achieving and maintaining employment.

IMPORTANT NOTE
This role requires strong organization, initiative, and follow-through.

You will help ensure the Raleigh program runs effectively, participants stay engaged, and placement and retention outcomes are achieved while supporting overall program operations.

Salary: $50,000–$52,000

new_jobs.title: PROGRAM COORDINATOR – WORKFORCE & OPERATIONS
new_jobs.description:
PROGRAM COORDINATOR – WORKFORCE & OPERATIONS (CHARLOTTE, NC)

Location:
Hybrid (with regular in-person presence based on program needs in Charlotte, NC)

COMPANY DESCRIPTION

Entryway is a national 501(c)(3) organization that transitions individuals and families out of homelessness into economic self-sufficiency. We partner with the $1.3 trillion multifamily housing industry to create pathways to stable employment, housing, and long-term opportunity.
As a catalyst for change, Entryway brings together property management companies, employers, service providers, and community partners to create a coordinated ecosystem of support. With more than 200 partner organizations nationwide, we deliver a proven, industry-powered model at the intersection of housing and employment.
Unlike traditional nonprofits that focus on either housing or employment, Entryway delivers both. Participants access stable jobs with a living wage, benefits, and on-site housing opportunities through our partners. Combined with training and individualized support, this approach creates the conditions for long-term success.
In North Carolina, the Program Coordinator in Charlotte plays a critical role in implementing this model—building partner relationships, guiding participants from recruitment through placement, and ensuring strong, measurable outcomes, including job placement and retention.

POSITION OVERVIEW

Entryway is seeking a highly organized, proactive, and detail-oriented Program Coordinator to lead and execute our workforce program in Charlotte.
This high-impact role is responsible for the full lifecycle of the program—from recruiting participants, to coordinating training programs, to driving placement and retention outcomes—while maintaining accurate tracking and supporting program operations.
The ideal candidate is organized, communicates effectively, takes ownership of results, and is comfortable balancing administrative work with participant and partner engagement in a fast-paced environment.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Program Coordination & Execution

- Coordinate and manage multi-week training programs, including securing instructors, managing logistics and schedules, and ensuring a high-quality participant experience 
- Build and maintain a pipeline of qualified participants through recruitment, screening, and onboarding 
- Ensure participants remain engaged, accountable, and on track through program completion 
Placement & Employer Engagement

- Build and maintain strong relationships with employer partners 
- Connect participants to job opportunities aligned with their skills and goals 
- Coordinate interviews and hiring processes 
- Drive placement outcomes and support early retention 
Participant Support

- Build strong, trust-based relationships with participants 
- Provide consistent communication, guidance, and accountability 
- Support resumes, applications, interview preparation, and next steps 
- Help address barriers to successful program completion 
Data Tracking & Reporting

- Maintain accurate, real-time tracking of participant data, attendance, and outcomes 
- Ensure documentation is complete and reporting requirements are met 
- Maintain organized, up-to-date systems and records 
Program Operations & Administrative Support

- Coordinate scheduling, communications, and program logistics 
- Maintain tracking tools and program materials 
- Draft communications and support outreach efforts 
- Assist with internal coordination as needed 
Ownership & Initiative

- Take ownership of program performance from recruitment through outcomes 
- Manage priorities with strong attention to detail 
- Identify challenges early and take initiative to resolve them 
- Follow through consistently and ensure accountability 

REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

- 2+ years of experience in program coordination, workforce development, or a related field 
- Strong organizational skills and attention to detail 
- Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines 
- Strong written and verbal communication skills 
- Experience with spreadsheets, databases, or tracking systems (Excel, Google Sheets, CRM, etc.) 

SUCCESS TRAITS

- Ownership mindset and strong follow-through 
- Proactive, solutions-oriented approach 
- Strong communicator across diverse audiences 
- Able to balance administration with participant engagement 
- Builds trust with individuals facing employment barriers 
- Highly organized and detail-oriented 
- Motivated to deliver measurable outcomes 

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience in workforce development, nonprofit programs, or social services 
- Experience working with underserved populations 
- Experience with job placement or employer engagement 
- Familiarity with CRM or case management systems 

This role directly impacts participants’ ability to achieve employment and housing stability by ensuring successful program completion, job placement, and continued employment.

IMPORTANT NOTE
This role requires a high level of ownership, organization, and accountability.

You will be responsible for ensuring the program runs effectively and consistently produces strong placement and retention outcomes while maintaining clear and accurate tracking.

Salary: $58,000–$60,000
